name: Chemical Engineering
description: Chemical engineering applies principles of chemistry, physics, mathematics, and economics to efficiently use, produce, design, transport, and transform energy and materials. It bridges molecular sciences and large-scale industrial processes across pharmaceuticals, petrochemicals, and food processing industries.

subjects.name: Mass Transfer
subjects.description: Study of mass transfer operations including diffusion, absorption, distillation, extraction, and drying. Covers Fick's law, mass transfer coefficients, and design of separation equipment used in chemical industries.
learning_outcomes: Apply Fick's law and mass transfer coefficients to diffusion problems
Design distillation and absorption columns for industrial separations
Analyze mass transfer operations using stage-wise and continuous contact methods
Evaluate the performance of drying, extraction, and crystallization equipment
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental to designing separation units in chemical, pharmaceutical, and petrochemical plants.

subjects.name: Heat Transfer
subjects.description: Comprehensive coverage of conduction, convection, and radiation heat transfer mechanisms. Includes heat exchanger design, evaporation, and thermal system analysis for chemical process industries.
learning_outcomes: Solve steady and unsteady heat conduction problems in various geometries
Design shell-and-tube and compact heat exchangers using NTU and LMTD methods
Analyze convective heat transfer with dimensional analysis and correlations
Evaluate radiation heat transfer between surfaces and in participating media
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for designing heat exchange equipment in refineries, power plants, and chemical reactors.

subjects.name: Fluid Mechanics
subjects.description: Study of fluid statics and dynamics including conservation laws, flow through pipes, pumps, compressors, and fluid moving machinery. Emphasizes applications in chemical process piping and transport of fluids.
learning_outcomes: Apply Bernoulli's equation and Navier-Stokes to analyze fluid flow systems
Design piping networks and select pumps for industrial fluid transport
Calculate pressure drops in laminar and turbulent flow regimes
Analyze flow measurement devices and fluid machinery performance
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for designing fluid transport systems in chemical plants, refineries, and water treatment facilities.

subjects.name: Chemical Thermodynamics
subjects.description: Application of thermodynamic principles to chemical systems including phase equilibria, chemical reaction equilibria, and property estimation. Covers equations of state, fugacity, activity coefficients, and Gibbs free energy analysis.
learning_outcomes: Apply laws of thermodynamics to closed and open chemical systems
Calculate phase equilibria using fugacity and activity coefficient models
Determine chemical reaction equilibrium compositions under various conditions
Use equations of state to predict PVT behavior of pure fluids and mixtures
subjects.industry_relevance: Indispensable for process simulation, reactor design, and separation process optimization in chemical industries.

subjects.name: Chemical Reaction Engineering
subjects.description: Design and analysis of chemical reactors including batch, CSTR, PFR, and packed bed reactors. Covers kinetics, rate laws, reactor sizing, multiple reactions, and non-isothermal reactor operation.
learning_outcomes: Develop rate expressions and design batch, CSTR, and PFR reactors for single reactions
Analyze multiple reactions to optimize selectivity and yield
Design non-isothermal reactors with heat effects and temperature profiles
Evaluate catalytic reactions and design heterogeneous reactor systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Core to designing reactors in petrochemical, pharmaceutical, and polymer manufacturing industries.

subjects.name: Process Control
subjects.description: Fundamentals of automatic process control including feedback, feedforward, and cascade control strategies. Covers transfer functions, PID controllers, stability analysis, and control valve instrumentation for chemical processes.
learning_outcomes: Develop transfer function models for chemical process dynamics
Design and tune PID controllers for single-loop and cascade control systems
Analyze closed-loop stability using root locus and frequency response methods
Implement advanced control strategies including feedforward and ratio control
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for automation and safe operation of chemical plants, refineries, and pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities.

subjects.name: Process Equipment Design
subjects.description: Mechanical design and fabrication aspects of chemical process equipment including pressure vessels, storage tanks, heat exchangers, distillation columns, and reactors. Covers ASME codes, material selection, and stress analysis.
learning_outcomes: Design pressure vessels and storage tanks per ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code
Perform mechanical design of heat exchangers, columns, and reactors
Select appropriate materials for corrosive and high-temperature process environments
Evaluate equipment costs and optimize designs for safety and reliability
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to equipment fabrication and plant construction in chemical, oil and gas, and fertilizer industries.

subjects.name: Separation Processes
subjects.description: Advanced separation techniques including membrane separation, adsorption, ion exchange, chromatography, and supercritical fluid extraction. Focuses on modern separation methods beyond conventional distillation and extraction.
learning_outcomes: Design membrane separation systems for gas and liquid applications
Analyze adsorption isotherms and design fixed-bed adsorption columns
Apply ion exchange and chromatography techniques for bioseparations
Evaluate supercritical fluid extraction and other emerging separation technologies
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for pharmaceutical purification, water treatment, gas processing, and biotechnology downstream operations.

subjects.name: Chemical Process Calculations
subjects.description: Introduction to material and energy balance calculations for chemical processes. Covers stoichiometry, recycle, bypass, purge streams, and combustion calculations fundamental to all chemical engineering analysis.
learning_outcomes: Perform material balance calculations for single and multiple unit processes
Apply energy balance principles to chemical processes including phase changes
Analyze recycle, bypass, and purge streams in continuous processes
Conduct combustion calculations and flue gas analysis
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational skill for all chemical engineering roles including process design, plant operation, and troubleshooting.

subjects.name: Transport Phenomena
subjects.description: Unified study of momentum, heat, and mass transfer at molecular and macroscopic levels. Uses shell balance and vector-tensor approaches to derive governing equations for transport processes in chemical systems.
learning_outcomes: Derive and solve shell balance equations for momentum, heat, and mass transport
Apply analogous transport principles across momentum, heat, and mass transfer
Develop mathematical models for coupled transport processes with chemical reactions
Analyze boundary layer phenomena and convective transport in complex geometries
subjects.industry_relevance: Advanced foundation for computational fluid dynamics, reactor modeling, and process simulation in chemical R&D.

subjects.name: Petroleum Refinery Engineering
subjects.description: Study of crude oil processing and refining operations including atmospheric and vacuum distillation, cracking, reforming, hydrotreating, and product blending. Covers refinery configuration, process integration, and petroleum product specifications.
learning_outcomes: Design crude distillation units and analyze refinery process configurations
Evaluate catalytic cracking, reforming, and hydrotreating reactor systems
Optimize product yields through process integration and blending strategies
Assess crude oil properties and their impact on refinery operation and economics
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to careers in petroleum refining, one of the largest sectors employing chemical engineers.

subjects.name: Petrochemical Technology
subjects.description: Production processes for major petrochemicals from olefins, aromatics, and synthesis gas. Covers steam cracking, catalytic reforming, and downstream processes for plastics, synthetic fibers, and specialty chemicals.
learning_outcomes: Describe steam cracking and catalytic reforming processes for petrochemical feedstocks
Design production processes for major olefins, aromatics, and their derivatives
Analyze process economics and technology selection for petrochemical complexes
Evaluate environmental and safety aspects of petrochemical production processes
subjects.industry_relevance: Core knowledge for careers in petrochemical manufacturing and integrated refinery-petrochemical complexes.

subjects.name: Polymer Technology
subjects.description: Polymer science and engineering covering polymerization mechanisms, reactor design for polymerization, polymer characterization, and processing techniques. Includes thermoplastics, thermosets, elastomers, and polymer composites.
learning_outcomes: Distinguish between addition, condensation, and coordination polymerization mechanisms
Design polymerization reactors accounting for kinetics, viscosity, and heat transfer
Analyze polymer characterization data including molecular weight and thermal properties
Evaluate polymer processing techniques including extrusion, injection molding, and blow molding
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for careers in plastics, rubber, coatings, and advanced materials industries.

subjects.name: Process Intensification
subjects.description: Novel approaches to dramatically reduce plant size and improve efficiency using intensified equipment and methods. Covers microreactors, spinning disc reactors, reactive distillation, membrane reactors, and ultrasonic/hydrodynamic cavitation.
learning_outcomes: Identify opportunities for process intensification in existing chemical processes
Design microreactor and spinning disc reactor systems for enhanced mass and heat transfer
Analyze hybrid separation-reaction systems such as reactive distillation and membrane reactors
Evaluate energy and cost savings from intensified versus conventional process designs
subjects.industry_relevance: Emerging field driving next-generation chemical plant design with smaller footprint and lower energy consumption.

subjects.name: Biochemical Engineering
subjects.description: Application of chemical engineering principles to biological systems including enzyme kinetics, bioreactor design, sterilization, and fermentation technology. Bridges chemical engineering with biotechnology and pharmaceutical manufacturing.
learning_outcomes: Apply Michaelis-Menten kinetics to enzyme-catalyzed reactions and inhibition analysis
Design bioreactors for microbial, mammalian, and plant cell cultures
Develop sterilization cycles and analyze aseptic operation of bioprocess equipment
Evaluate mass transfer limitations in aerobic fermentation systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for careers in biopharmaceuticals, industrial biotechnology, and biofuel production.

subjects.name: Safety in Chemical Industry
subjects.description: Chemical process safety covering hazard identification, risk assessment, consequence analysis, and safety instrumented systems. Includes HAZOP, LOPA, QRA, fire and explosion modeling, and toxic release dispersion analysis.
learning_outcomes: Conduct HAZOP studies and LOPA for identifying process hazards
Perform consequence analysis for fires, explosions, and toxic releases
Design safety instrumented systems and relief devices for overpressure protection
Apply risk assessment methodologies including QRA to chemical plants
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for compliance with safety regulations and preventing industrial accidents in chemical process industries.

subjects.name: Environmental Engineering
subjects.description: Environmental aspects of chemical engineering including air pollution control, wastewater treatment, solid waste management, and environmental impact assessment. Covers regulatory frameworks and green engineering principles.
learning_outcomes: Design air pollution control systems including scrubbers, cyclones, and electrostatic precipitators
Apply biological and physicochemical treatment processes for industrial wastewater
Evaluate environmental impact assessments and life cycle analyses for chemical processes
Develop waste minimization and green chemistry strategies for sustainable manufacturing
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand due to environmental regulations and corporate sustainability initiatives in all chemical sectors.

subjects.name: Plant Design & Economics
subjects.description: Comprehensive plant design integrating process flow diagrams, P&IDs, equipment selection, and economic analysis. Covers capital cost estimation, profitability analysis, optimization, and plant layout for chemical manufacturing facilities.
learning_outcomes: Develop process flow diagrams and piping and instrumentation diagrams for chemical plants
Estimate capital and operating costs using factored and detailed estimation methods
Evaluate project profitability using NPV, IRR, payback period, and sensitivity analysis
Optimize plant layout and process configuration for minimum cost and maximum safety
subjects.industry_relevance: Culminating subject that prepares students for real-world plant design and project management roles.
